title: Steamy French Squid Stir-fry
description: A comforting and whole30-friendly French dish featuring baby squid cooked in a steam-based stir-fry with nutty flavors.
instructions: Place the squid in a food processor with 4 cloves of garlic and blend until you get a paste. Season with salt.
Wash and pat dry the squid. Spread it out on a large plate lined with parchment paper.
Brush some olive oil over the squid, then use your hands to coat evenly with the garlic-squid mixture. Let rest for 5 minutes.
While the squid is resting, heat a heavy-bottomed skillet over medium-high heat. Add enough olive oil to coat the bottom of the pan.
Once hot, add the remaining garlic and ginger slices. Stir-fry for about 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add in the cubed green peppers and red bell pepper, stirring occasionally for about 2-3 minutes until slightly softened.
Increase heat to high, carefully transfer the squid onto the skillet (do not overcrowd), and stir-fry for an additional 5 minutes or until it turns golden brown.
Add scallion whites at the last minute. Stir fry for another 1-2 minutes just to wilt them slightly.
Remove from heat immediately and serve hot with a sprinkle of fresh herbs if desired.
